## Cases mentioned in passing

### Purdue University charcoal briquette contest liquid oxygen incident
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Purdue University charcoal briquette contest liquid oxygen incident"
- **Anchor:** `§5.p5`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "If you really want to save money, you actually start out with a nitrogen-oxygen mixture, but then you don't want the high nitrogen in most cases, so you switch over to argon-oxygen, and that way you don't have to use as much expensive argon. Steel mills are using so much liquid oxygen and liquid argon that Linde will come and build a refrigeration plant to liquefy air right there next door and pipe it right over to your mill — a six-inch line, a lot of liquid oxygen. The only people who use more liquid oxygen in shorter periods of time is the U.S. Air Force sending rockets off. Burning oxygen..."
